steps.title: Find the slope from the two given points
steps.content:
Using the points from the table, $(-12, -45)$ and $(6, 45)$, apply the slope formula:

$$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{45 - (-45)}{6 - (-12)} = \frac{90}{18} = 5$$

steps.title: Write the equation of the line
steps.content:
Using point-slope form with point $(6, 45)$ and slope $m = 5$:

$$y - 45 = 5(x - 6)$$
$$y = 5x + 15$$
